item.text: This is a small Vite app, so I’m reading the current entry point and styles before editing. The likely path is a single React component plus CSS, with build verification afterward.

I found a bare React/Vite starter. I’m going to replace the placeholder with a self-contained simulation: metric events spawn labeled bars, physics updates them into stacks, and controls will gate the animation loop and reduced-motion behavior.

The edit will replace the starter with a real-time metric rain view: simulated events enter a feed, each event becomes a patterned labeled bar, and the animation loop handles falling and stack collision. Reduced-motion will still accept events but place bars without continuous falling.
The main implementation is in place. I’m checking the TypeScript/build path now; if the compiler catches anything, I’ll tighten it before calling the task done.
